golden_categories.name: Basic Email Validation
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating standard email formats and basic rules.

golden_categories.name: Domain-Specific Validation
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with domain-specific rules.

golden_categories.name: Underscore in Domain
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with underscores allowed in the domain.

golden_categories.name: UTF8 Local Part Restriction
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses without UTF8 characters in the local part.

golden_categories.name: Display Name Support
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with optional display names.

golden_categories.name: Required Display Name
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with mandatory display names.

golden_categories.name: IP Domain Validation
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with IPs allowed as domains.

golden_categories.name: Blacklisted Characters
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with restrictions on specific characters in the name.

golden_categories.name: Max Length Restriction
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with maximum length restrictions.

golden_categories.name: Ignore Max Length
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with maximum length ignored.

golden_categories.name: Blacklisted Domains
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with denylisted domains.

golden_categories.name: Blacklist with Regular Expressions
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with domain blacklist using regular expressions.

golden_categories.name: Whitelisted Domains
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with allowed domains.

golden_categories.name: Whitelist with Regular Expressions
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with domain whitelist using regular expressions.

generated_categories.name: Basic Email Validation
generated_categories.description: Tests the core functionality of email validation, including default behavior and common valid/invalid cases.

generated_categories.name: Display Name Handling
generated_categories.description: Tests validation of emails with display names, including allowing and requiring display names.

generated_categories.name: Domain Validation
generated_categories.description: Tests validation of email domains, including allowing IP domains and requiring top-level domains.

generated_categories.name: Character Restrictions
generated_categories.description: Tests validation of emails with restrictions on characters in the local part, including blacklisted characters and disallowed UTF-8 characters.

generated_categories.name: Host Restrictions
generated_categories.description: Tests validation of emails with restrictions on specific host domains using a blacklist.

golden_to_generated.reason: Both categories focus on basic email validation, covering standard formats and common valid/invalid cases.
GENC-3 includes domain-specific validation but also covers IP domains and TLD requirements, which are outside the scope of GC-2.
No generated category explicitly addresses email validation with underscores in the domain.
GENC-4 includes UTF-8 restrictions but also covers blacklisted characters, which are unrelated to GC-4.
GENC-2 explicitly addresses emails with optional display names, aligning with GC-5.
GENC-2 explicitly addresses emails with mandatory display names, aligning with GC-6.
GENC-3 includes IP domain validation but also covers unrelated domain-specific rules.
GENC-4 includes blacklisted character restrictions but also covers UTF-8 restrictions, which are unrelated to GC-8.
No generated category explicitly addresses maximum length restrictions for email validation.
No generated category explicitly addresses ignoring maximum length restrictions for email validation.
GENC-5 explicitly addresses email validation with blacklisted domains, aligning with GC-11.
GENC-5 addresses blacklisted domains but does not explicitly mention regular expressions, which are a key aspect of GC-12.
No generated category explicitly addresses whitelisted domains for email validation.
No generated category explicitly addresses whitelisted domains with regular expressions for email validation.

generated_to_golden.reason: GENC-1 aligns with GC-1 as both focus on basic email validation, covering standard formats and common valid/invalid cases.
GENC-2 covers both optional and mandatory display name validation, aligning with GC-5 and GC-6.
GENC-3 includes domain-specific validation and IP domain validation, partially aligning with GC-2 and GC-7.
GENC-4 includes UTF-8 restrictions and blacklisted character restrictions, partially aligning with GC-4 and GC-8.
GENC-5 addresses blacklisted domains but does not explicitly mention regular expressions, which are key to GC-12.
